dialogueChunks.intro: I almost don't dare say it out loud, {player_name}, but I think the worst is finally behind us.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The blight is receding. The alchemist applied the compound you brought back, and the soil is responding. {target_npc} planted a test row this morning, and by afternoon there were green shoots pushing through. Green, {player_name}. After weeks of nothing but black and rot. The settlement is cautiously, carefully, allowing itself to hope again.
dialogueChunks.outro: You fought nature itself to save us. Take this: it's all we have, but we give it with full hearts.

playerResponses.text: Green shoots from blackened soil. That's the most beautiful thing I've heard in weeks.
I'm glad the treatment worked. May your harvest be strong.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, sit with me a moment. I want to tell you what happened after you left.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The sickness broke three days ago. Just like that: one morning, people woke up and the fever was gone. The alchemist says the compound you brought tipped the balance at exactly the right moment. {target_npc} was the last to recover, and the first thing they did was walk to the well and drink a full cup of water without coughing. The whole square went silent watching them. Then someone started clapping, and it spread until every person in {location} was applauding a person drinking water.
dialogueChunks.outro: We cheered for a cup of water, {player_name}. That's how far we'd fallen, and how far you lifted us. Take this.

playerResponses.text: Applause for a cup of water. I'll remember that for a long time.
Simple things matter most after you almost lose them. Glad I could help.

dialogueChunks.intro: The road is open, {player_name}. A merchant arrived this morning: the first in over a month.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: You should have seen the faces when that cart rolled through the gate. People ran out of their houses. {target_npc} actually embraced the merchant, who was thoroughly confused by the welcome. The supplies the merchant brought are already being distributed, and the trade route you cleared is holding. {location} is connected to the world again. The isolation is over, and the slow starvation with it.
dialogueChunks.outro: Here. Every family contributed to this. You broke the curse of misfortune that had been strangling us for months.

playerResponses.text: Not a curse: just bad luck that finally ran out. Keep the road maintained.
A merchant at the gates. Civilization returns. Good.

dialogueChunks.intro: I never thought I'd say this, but good things are happening, {player_name}. Actual, genuine good things.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The new mine vein {target_npc} found is producing twice what the old one ever did. The ore quality is exceptional, and the miners are saying it could sustain {location} for decades. What started as the worst disaster in our history has led us to something better than what we had before. The old miners have a saying: 'The mountain takes with one hand and gives with the other.' It took a while for the giving hand to open, but open it did.
dialogueChunks.outro: From ruin to riches. And it started with you refusing to let misfortune have the last word. Take this with our profound gratitude.

playerResponses.text: From tragedy to something better. That's a story worth telling.
I'm glad the mountain decided to give back. Mine it wisely.
